message:
test(fcp-core): pin LiveStatus (DescriptorStatus) serde tag matrix across JSON + CBOR

Lands the pinning test asked for by bead flywheel_connectors-l81wi
("LiveStatus serde tag matrix"). The bead's "LiveStatus" name maps to
the public DescriptorStatus enum in fcp-core (re-imported under the
LiveStatus alias inside the test); this test pins the on-the-wire
discriminator for each variant in lockstep across both encodings.

Test (crates/fcp-core/tests/live_status_serde_tag_matrix.rs, new):

- LiveStatusCase fixture binds three pieces per variant: the enum
  value, the canonical JSON tag (`"ready"`, `"degraded"`, `"failed"`,
  …), and the deterministic CBOR hex byte sequence the canonical
  ciborium encoder emits. Constants are stored in a const slice so
  adding a variant means appending one row — accidental drift on
  any of the three bindings becomes a hard test failure.
- For each row, the test asserts:
  * serde_json::to_string roundtrips the exact JSON tag and the
    parsed value re-equals the input.
  * ciborium encoding produces the exact frozen byte sequence and
    decoding the same bytes returns the original variant.
- Header comment records the bead-text vs. type-name reconciliation
  ("LiveStatus" in the bead → DescriptorStatus in the codebase) so
  future readers don't waste a grep on a non-existent type.

No production code changes — pure pinning test against the existing
DescriptorStatus surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
